symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged crushing central chest pain at rest (>20 min)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest pain of ischaemic character lasting more than 20 minutes is the defining feature of infarction rather than stable angina.
Reflux can cause central chest pain but rarely with this severity, duration and autonomic accompaniment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation to the left arm (numb) and jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiation to the left arm and jaw markedly increases the likelihood of cardiac ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diaphoresis, pallor, nausea and vomiting (autonomic activation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Drenching sweat with nausea/vomiting reflects intense sympathetic and vagal activation typical of a large ischaemic event.
Reflux does not produce diaphoresis and pallor of this degree.
Dyspnoea with ACS suggests early ischaemic left-ventricular dysfunction and pulmonary congestion.
Breathlessness is also a feature of PE, which is kept live as a mimic.
A sense of impending doom is a recognised accompaniment of major cardiovascular catastrophe and should not be dismissed as anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain not relieved by rest or position change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to settle with rest distinguishes infarction from stable exertional angina and from musculoskeletal/pleuritic pain.
Observable pallor and sweating support a serious cardiac event; here they are patient/carer-observable over video, so admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fourth heart sound (S4) gallop at the apex

investigation_details.expected_result: S4 gallop reflecting reduced ventricular compliance from acute ischaemia; no new murmur or rub
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sign of acute ischaemic ventricular stiffening is unavailable; requires an in-person clinician and cannot be obtained via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An S4 is consistent with acute ischaemic ventricular dysfunction — a clinician-auscultated sign, sealed from telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Mild bibasal crackles indicating early pulmonary congestion from ischaemic left-ventricular dysfunction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Early heart-failure sign not detectable remotely; requires in-person examination.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction with left-ventricular dysfunction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bibasal crackles indicate incipient cardiogenic pulmonary congestion — a clinician-obtained finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals — HR 110, BP 100/65, RR 24, SpO2 94%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction with haemodynamic strain
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia with borderline-low blood pressure, tachypnoea and mild hypoxaemia indicate haemodynamic strain; these are clinician/device measurements not reliably obtainable via telehealth in this household (no home device reported).

investigation_details.expected_result: Dynamic ST-segment changes (ST-elevation suspected) localising the ischaemic territory; drives the primary-PCI decision
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis and reperfusion decision cannot be made; must be performed by paramedics/ED, never attempted via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ST-elevation on the ECG is the diagnostic trigger for primary PCI; obtainable only by the emergency team.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated / rising troponin confirming myocardial necrosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Biochemical confirmation of infarction is unavailable; a hospital blood test never obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serial troponin confirms or excludes myocardial necrosis and is a hospital investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Prolonged rest pain, classic radiation, autonomic collapse, impending doom and failure to relieve with rest together make an ischaemic emergency overwhelmingly likely and mandate immediate 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The time-critical diagnostic test that identifies ST-elevation and triggers primary PCI — performed by the emergency team, never via telehealth, and must not delay the ambulance.
Confirms myocardial necrosis in hospital; complements the ECG.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/telehealth attempt to obtain or interpret an ECG remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rhythm/ischaemia characterisation cannot be attempted over telehealth and must never delay emergency transfer.
